# Agenticfeed — github.com/bluestratus/agenticfeed

> A lightweight open specification for making ecommerce product catalogues readable by AI shopping agents.

The Agenticfeed Standard defines how merchants expose structured product data for AI agents to discover, reason about, and recommend products in conversational shopping contexts.

## What this repository contains

- [README.md](README.md) — full specification
- [examples/feed.json](examples/feed.json) — feed index (DataFeed, schema.org)
- [examples/product.json](examples/product.json) — product detail (Product, schema.org)
- [examples/questions.json](examples/questions.json) — buyer questions endpoint (ItemList of Question)
- [examples/problems.json](examples/problems.json) — buyer problems endpoint (ItemList)
- [examples/use-cases.json](examples/use-cases.json) — use cases endpoint (ItemList)
- [examples/link-tag.html](examples/link-tag.html) — discovery tag snippet

## Key concepts

- Merchants add `<link rel="agenticfeed" type="application/json" href="...">` to their page `<head>` for autodiscovery
- The feed index is a schema.org `DataFeed` pointing to intent endpoints
- Intent endpoints are schema.org `ItemList` documents containing `Question` and `ListItem` entries linked to products
- Product detail documents are schema.org `Product` with an `agenticfeed` namespace for questions, problems, and use cases
- All product URLs include UTM parameters for attribution tracking

## Reference implementation

The reference implementation is available at https://agenticfeed.ai

Agenticfeed automatically generates intent data (questions, problems, use cases) for merchant product catalogues using AI, publishes feeds conforming to this standard, and handles daily stock synchronisation.
